diagrams.title: Contiguous Memory Allocation Layout
diagrams.description: Shows a memory layout with multiple processes stored in contiguous blocks with free holes between them
diagrams.purpose: Understand how processes are laid out contiguously in memory and identify free holes that cause fragmentation

diagrams.title: Internal vs External Fragmentation
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing internal fragmentation inside a partition and external fragmentation as scattered free holes
elements: Internal: Partition 100KB with 70KB process, 30KB wasted inside
External: Three processes with scattered holes of 20KB, 30KB, 15KB between them
diagrams.purpose: Visual distinction between the two types of memory fragmentation

diagrams.title: Address Translation using Base Register
diagrams.description: Shows translation from logical address to physical address using base register addition
diagrams.purpose: Understand dynamic address translation in contiguous allocation

diagrams.title: Memory Compaction Process
diagrams.description: Before and after compaction showing how scattered free memory is combined into one large block
elements: Before: Process A, Hole, Process B, Hole, Process C, scattered holes
After: Process A, Process B, Process C, One large combined hole
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how compaction reduces external fragmentation by moving processes together
